name: Kaban Wind Farm
notable: 37x Vestas V117-4.2MW. 100MW/200MWh BESS co-located. Neoen developed. Powerlink SynCon for system strength. Far North QLD.

timeline.title: QLD development approval
timeline.detail: State Development department granted approval for Kaban Green Power Hub

timeline.title: Financial close — A$373M
timeline.detail: Neoen completed financing; Vestas contracted for EPC

timeline.title: Turbine components arrive
timeline.detail: Vestas V162-5.6 MW components arrive at Port of Cairns

timeline.title: Commercial operations commenced
timeline.detail: Commencement date from AEMO registration via OpenElectricity.

timeline.title: First generation recorded
timeline.detail: First market dispatch data recorded in AEMO systems.

eis_specs.document_title: Kaban Green Power Hub EIS — Coordinator-General's Report (QLD DSDILGP)
eis_specs.hub_height_note: Standard 91.5 m hub for V117-4.2 MW on Far North QLD Tablelands
eis_specs.wind_speed_period: Mean annual wind speed at hub height; Kaban area, Atherton Tablelands, Far North QLD
eis_specs.connection_substation_name: Kaban 132 kV substation (new on-site), shared Tablelands 132 kV corridor with Mount Emerald
eis_specs.connection_distance_note: ~15 km to Powerlink 132 kV network. Shared transmission infrastructure with adjacent Mount Emerald reduces per-project connection cost.
eis_specs.connection_augmentation: Kaban and Mount Emerald together required combined ~333 MW upgrade to Powerlink Tablelands 132 kV network.
eis_specs.notes: 152 MW (36 x 4.2 MW Vestas V117), Atherton Tablelands, Far North QLD (near Ravenshoe). Developer: Windlab (sold to Pacific Hydro / PowAR). QLD EIS approved 2018. Now operating (2023). Adjacent to Mount Emerald Wind Farm; shares 132 kV Powerlink connection corridor. V117-4.2 MW is a high-efficiency variant of the V117 platform.
